name: Aadiyan Dubey
title: Founder & CEO
quote: Our journey has always been about empowering those who make our cities run. As we evolve, our responsibility to build a permanent, resilient, and sustainable ecosystem only deepens.
letterTitle: A note from our Founder, Kabir Sharma.
introduction:
Dear Shareholders, Partners, and Friends,

When we first wrote the code for our delivery platform in 2018, we had a simple, singular goal: to help local restaurants reach customers online. We didn't anticipate how deeply integrated we would become in the daily lives of millions of people, or how our operations would expand to encompass grocery delivery and merchant business software.

Today, our business is no longer just food delivery. It is hyper-local commerce in its entirety.

Over the past few years, we have operated several independent brands. We acquired and scaled Janhal, turning it into India's fastest-growing quick-commerce network, delivering fresh produce and daily essentials to households in under 10 minutes. We built Local Branding Software, a powerful merchant SaaS suite that empowers over 250,000 local store owners to manage their digital presence, run hyper-local campaigns, and gain deep customer insights.

Internally, we have always viewed ourselves as a single, cohesive team working toward a shared mission. However, our corporate structure and external communication have remained fragmented.

To align our long-term vision and build an institution that endures, our Board of Directors has approved the transition of our corporate entity to a unified name: LocalSM Limited.
calloutQuote: LocalSM isn't just a name. It's a mission statement.
middleText:
The name "LocalSM" represents our absolute commitment to the hyper-local ecosystem. The "Local" represents our roots—the neighborhood grocery store, the local bakery, the delivery partner on the street. The "SM" stands for Scale and Management—our promise to provide the world-class technology, logistics, and software infrastructure to help these local heroes scale and thrive in a digital world.

I want to make it clear that this is a corporate-level change. Our consumer-facing applications—the apps you use to order food or groceries—will retain their familiar brands. LocalSM will serve as the parent corporate identity, uniting our three core pillars:

pillars.title: LocalSM Delivery
pillars.description: Connecting diners and restaurants across 800+ cities.

pillars.title: Janhal
pillars.description: Groceries and essentials delivered in minutes.

conclusion:
Our mission remains unchanged: to endure, evolve, and empower.

We are not building a company for the next quarter or the next fiscal year. We are building an institution that will stand for decades. This requires extreme operational discipline, a relentless focus on first-principles thinking, and a willingness to disrupt our own business models before others do.

As we embark on this next chapter as LocalSM, our focus on sustainability and responsibility will only deepen. We are accelerating our transition to an all-electric delivery fleet by 2030, and we are working toward absolute net-zero emissions across our value chain by 2033.

Thank you for your continued trust, partnership, and belief in our mission.
message:
Every great company begins with a simple belief.
Mine began with a question: Why should technology only work for large businesses when millions of local businesses still struggle to grow?
I started this journey with a vision to build products that solve real problems—not products that simply follow trends. That vision became the foundation of everything we build today.
Technology has transformed the world, but many local businesses, students, entrepreneurs, and communities have been left behind. They deserve tools that are powerful, affordable, and easy to use. They deserve opportunities that are not limited by geography or resources.
That belief led to the creation of LocalSM.
LocalSM was never meant to be just another marketplace. It was designed as an ecosystem where technology empowers local commerce, creates employment, supports entrepreneurs, and builds trust between businesses and customers. Every feature we design is guided by one principle: create genuine value for the people who use it.
Building a startup is never a straight path.
There have been countless late nights, failed experiments, rejected ideas, technical challenges, financial limitations, and moments where success seemed uncertain. Every obstacle became a lesson. Every setback strengthened our determination. Instead of slowing us down, challenges refined our vision.
Our journey has always been driven by execution rather than promises.
We believe that products should speak louder than presentations. Innovation is not about adding complexity; it is about making life simpler. Great technology should disappear into the background while solving meaningful problems for people.
As we grew, our vision expanded beyond a single platform.
Today, we are building a broader ecosystem under Pnazo—a company focused on creating products that combine technology, design, artificial intelligence, commerce, and digital infrastructure. LocalSM is one of the first major products in this ecosystem, but it is only the beginning.
Our ambition is not to copy what already exists.
We want to build products that people genuinely enjoy using. Products that are beautifully designed, technically excellent, and capable of solving problems at scale. We believe Indian companies can create world-class technology that competes globally while remaining deeply connected to local communities.
Culture matters as much as code.
We encourage curiosity, ownership, continuous learning, and the courage to challenge conventional thinking. Titles do not build companies—people do. Every intern, engineer, designer, marketer, and team member contributes to shaping the future of our products.
We are committed to creating an environment where talent is valued more than background, ideas matter more than hierarchy, and execution matters more than words.
Trust is another principle that defines everything we do.
Whether it is protecting user data, building reliable systems, supporting our partners, or creating transparent processes, trust is something that must be earned every day. We know that lasting companies are built not only through innovation but through responsibility.
Artificial intelligence represents one of the most significant technological shifts of our generation.
Our goal is not simply to use AI because it is popular. Our goal is to integrate intelligence into products where it creates measurable value—helping businesses grow faster, helping users make better decisions, and removing unnecessary complexity from everyday work.
We also recognize our responsibility as builders.
Technology influences lives, economies, and societies. Every product decision carries consequences. We strive to build responsibly, keeping accessibility, privacy, security, and long-term impact at the center of our decisions.
The road ahead is ambitious.
We envision products serving millions of users, empowering businesses across cities and villages, creating new employment opportunities, enabling entrepreneurs, and demonstrating that meaningful innovation can emerge from determined teams with bold ideas.
Success will never be measured only by revenue, funding, or valuation.
It will be measured by the businesses that grow because of our platforms, the people who find opportunities through our ecosystem, the communities strengthened by technology, and the trust we earn from every customer.
To everyone who has joined us—our users, partners, investors, mentors, employees, interns, and supporters—thank you for believing in our vision. Your trust motivates us to build better every single day.
We are still at the beginning of our journey.
The challenges ahead are significant, but so are the opportunities. We will continue learning, innovating, and building with the same determination that started this company.
The future belongs to those who create it.
We intend to keep building.
